_comment: Investigation for #811: which rank gets knocked/yanked backward -- the front (engaged) rank or a rear rank -- under sustained heavy melee. Authored fresh (not a copy of demos/inputs/all-out-attack.json): a different seed, a smaller (96-soldier, 13-file) Infantry line further left on the field, and a different pre-clash hold so the two lines close at a different point. Selects the player unit (uid 0) and arms both the soldier-ID overlay (Settings.show_soldier_ids, via #815's new set_show_soldier_ids_session) and the engaged-soldier highlight (Settings.show_engaged_highlight) so a labeled-but-not-amber soldier that visibly separates toward the rear is the tell. Camera holds a wide establishing frame, then eases into a tight zoom on the clash point once the lines are about to meet (past LOD_ZOOM_IN=1.55, so show_soldier_ids actually renders) -- one hold, one eased move, one long hold, per the no-jerky-camera convention.
